attacking_style: Explosive attacks on punchy final climbs (observed winning from late attack with Roglic on final climb). Also opportunistic in technical finales under 3km when positioned for sprint or breakaway scenarios.
team_tactics: Versatile role: deployed as leadout man for Viviani but converts to personal opportunities when presented. Not always the protected rider, which creates opportunistic winning chances.
terrain_preference: Punchy slopes where he can drop pure climbers (dropped Quintana) combined with technical descents/finales that favor bike handling. Sprint-finish capability after hard efforts distinguishes him from pure climbers.
vulnerability: Sprint timing errors in reduced bunch scenarios - opened too early from breakaway chase allowing stronger finisher (van Aert) to come over. May lack sustained climbing power for long mountain stages.
key_patterns: Converts leadout/domestique positioning into personal wins opportunistically when designated leader falters (2 instances)
Strong in technical finales under 3km, both sprint and post-descent scenarios (2 observations)
Can outsprint pure climbers after punchy climbs but vulnerable to mistimed efforts against specialists
Effective in reduced bunch/breakaway scenarios but sprint execution inconsistent compared to pure sprinters
form_trajectory: neutral: Mixed 2019 results show maintained opportunism (Tirreno win) but execution errors emerging (Dauphine sprint timing). No recent data to confirm trend.
